root_motivation: Make 'the LLM cannot change a value' structurally true by housing query results in a bounded server-side store keyed by opaque handles — so the context window never contains mutable data, and a memory-constrained host (0 swap, ~2.3 GB free) cannot OOM from an unbounded result cache.
user_persona: A Rust library consumer (mqo-mcp-server, dh-ops, dh-mcp-server) that calls Store::put / ::get / ::derive / ::lineage / ::evict_expired / ::stats to manage in-memory query results on a single-process server under a total-bytes cap.

unfakeable_metric.name: tests_pass_count
unfakeable_metric.harness_command: cargo test --workspace --release 2>&1 | grep -E '^test result' | awk '{sum += $4} END {print sum}'

acceptance_criteria.description: put then get returns the same dataset; the handle string contains none of the column names or row values.
derive(parent, ...) returns a NEW handle, leaves the parent retrievable unchanged, and lineage(child) includes the parent (immutability + provenance).
A dataset past its TTL returns Expired (distinct from NotFound) after evict_expired().
Inserting datasets past max_total_bytes evicts LRU entries until under cap; the most-recently-used dataset survives; total_bytes <= cap.
There is no public mutation API — derive is the only path to a changed dataset; compile-time + runtime test.
stats() reports live dataset count + total bytes accurately after a mixed put/derive/evict sequence.
cargo test --release passes; cargo clippy --release -- -D warnings clean.
acceptance_criteria.test: cargo test --release exit 0 AND cargo clippy --release -- -D warnings exit 0

scope: Rust library crate `dh-store` with Dataset (columnar Vec-of-typed-Vec, no Arrow dep), Store (put/get/derive/lineage/evict_expired/stats), and LookupError
Opaque handle IDs minted as UUID v4 prefixed hdl_ (unguessable, content-blind)
Per-entry TTL with tombstone Expired distinction from NotFound after evict_expired
LRU + max_total_bytes cap with two-pass eviction (skip lineage parents first, force second pass if needed)
Immutable derive semantics: no mutate/update API; derive always allocates a new handle and records Lineage
Thread-safety via Mutex<StoreInner> (Arc<Store> shareable across threads)
non_goals: No Arrow / Parquet / columnar serialization — plain Rust enums + Vec only
No persistence — store is entirely in-memory; restarting the process drops all datasets
No distributed or cross-process replication — single-process server only
No query execution — dh-store holds results; dh-ops executes operations against them
No async API — Mutex is acceptable for v1 single-process server; RwLock/async is a v2 concern
No gRPC/HTTP server — pure library crate; embedding is the consumer's responsibility

additional.max_total_bytes_constraint: must be configurable and default-safe; 0 means unlimited (not recommended)

five_whys_trace.q: Why does mqo-mcp-server hand Vec<Value> straight to the caller instead of returning a handle?
five_whys_trace.a: There was no server-side store for it to put the bytes into — the handle concept existed in dh-spec but nowhere to actually hold the data.

five_whys_trace.q: Why does it matter that bytes live server-side rather than in the context window?
five_whys_trace.a: An LLM with query results in its context can hallucinate modified values; a handle is opaque so the LLM can reference but never alter the underlying bytes.

five_whys_trace.q: Why is a bounded memory footprint non-optional here specifically?
five_whys_trace.a: The target host has 0 swap and ~2.3 GB free (measured 2026-06-08); an unbounded result cache would OOM the entire server.

five_whys_trace.q: Why must eviction preserve lineage parents?
five_whys_trace.a: An operation that derived dataset B from A must be auditable — if A disappears silently, provenance is lost and downstream ops on B become unverifiable.

five_whys_trace.q: Why is immutability (no mutate API) the design principle rather than, say, versioning?
five_whys_trace.a: Structural immutability makes the 'LLM cannot change a value' guarantee mechanically true rather than a runtime check — there is no code path through which mutation could happen.

ambiguities_resolved.question: Should get() auto-evict expired entries, or only evict_expired()?
ambiguities_resolved.resolution: evict_expired() is the explicit sweep. get() on an entry that has not been swept returns the entry if still present (no lazy expiry at read time). This keeps get() deterministic.

ambiguities_resolved.question: What byte_estimate strategy for Dataset?
ambiguities_resolved.resolution: Sum of ColumnData::byte_estimate() per column: sizeof(Option<T>) * len for fixed-width types, sizeof(Option<String>) + str.len() per slot for string types. Heap-only; does not count struct stack overhead. Acceptable precision for a size cap.
